heading: Special rules
label: 52 Special rules
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990, referred to in subsec. (e), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 101–508Nov. 5, 1990

Amendments2011—Subsec. (c). designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted heading, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 112–561997—Subsec. (c). substituted “work opportunity credit” for “targeted jobs credit”.Pub. L. 105–341996—Subsec. (e)(1) to (3). redesignated pars. (2) and (3) as (1) and (2), respectively, and struck out former par. (1) which read as follows: “an organization to which section 593 (relating to reserves for losses on loans) applies,”.Pub. L. 104–1881990—Subsec. L. 101–5081984—Subsec. (a). , substituted “the credit (if any) determined under section 51(a) with respect to each such member” for “the credit (if any) allowable by section 44B to each such member”.Pub. (c). , substituted “credit shall be allowed under section 38 for any targeted jobs credit determined under this subpart” for “credit shall be allowed under section 44B”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 474(p)(6)Subsec. (d)(2). , substituted “, subject to section 38(c), a credit under section 38(a)” for “, subject to section 53 a credit under section 44B”.Pub. (e). , (C), redesignated subsec. (g) as (e) and struck out par. (3) which provided that the $100,000 amount specified in section 51(d) applicable to such estate or trust be reduced to an amount which bears the same ratio to $100,000 as the portion of the credit allocable to the estate or trust under paragraph (1) bears to the entire amount of such credit. Former subsec. (e), which related to a change in status from self-employed to employee, was struck out.Pub. L. 95–600, § 321(c)(1)(A)Subsecs. (f) to (h). , redesignated subsecs. (f) to (h) as (d) to (f), respectively.Pub. L. 95–600, § 321(c)(1)(A)Subsec. (i). , struck out subsec. (i) which related to a $50,000 limitation in the case of married individuals filing separate returns.Pub. L. 95–600, § 321(c)(1)(A)(i)Subsec. (j). , struck out subsec. (j) which related to certain short taxable years.Pub. L. 95–600, § 321(c)(1)(A)(i)

children.text: “more than 50 percent” shall be substituted for “at least 80 percent” each place it appears in section 1563(a)(1), and
the determination shall be made without regard to subsections (a)(4) and (e)(3)(C) of section 1563.

children.heading: Employees of partnerships, proprietorships, etc., which are under common control
children.label: b Employees of partnerships, proprietorships, etc., which are under common control

children.text: all employees of trades or business (whether or not incorporated) which are under common control shall be treated as employed by a single employer, and
the credit (if any) determined under section 51(a) with respect to each trade or business shall be its proportionate share of the wages giving rise to such credit.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under section 38 for any work opportunity credit determined under this subpart to any organization (other than a cooperative described in section 521) which is exempt from income tax under this chapter.

children.heading: Credit made available to qualified tax-exempt organizations employing qualified veterans
children.label: 2 Credit made available to qualified tax-exempt organizations employing qualified veterans
children.text: For credit against payroll taxes for employment of qualified veterans by qualified tax-exempt organizations, see section 3111(e).

the amount of the credit determined under this subpart for any taxable year shall be apportioned between the estate or trust and the beneficiaries on the basis of the income of the estate or trust allocable to each, and
any beneficiary to whom any amount has been apportioned under paragraph (1) shall be allowed, subject to section 38(c), a credit under section 38(a) for such amount.

children.heading: Limitations with respect to certain persons
children.label: e Limitations with respect to certain persons

children.text: a regulated investment company or a real estate investment trust subject to taxation under subchapter M (section 851 and following), and
a cooperative organization described in section 1381(a),
